title: Pyrus khasiana
taxonomy: Pyrus khasiana is a species of pear tree in the family Rosaceae. It is native to the Himalayan region and is often classified as a wild pear. The species is closely related to other pear species, such as Pyrus communis, and shares similar characteristics. Pyrus khasiana is typically classified as a deciduous tree, with a broad leaf shape and a rounded crown. Its classification as a species is further supported by its unique genetic makeup and distinct physical features.

intro: Pyrus khasiana, also known as the Himalayan pear, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus khasiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities.
